questions.question: What does accuracy measure?
options: How close to the true/correct value
questions.explanation: Accuracy = closeness to the TRUE value. It's about being RIGHT, not about exactness.

questions.question: You can judge if a measurement is accurate without knowing the true value.
questions.explanation: False. You NEED the true value to compare against. Without it, you can't know if you're close or far off.

questions.question: A scale reads 65.3 kg when true weight is 65.2 kg. Accurate?
questions.explanation: Yes! Only 0.1 kg off. Very close to the true value = accurate.
